metadata.title: Build an Orbital Energy Diagram with Claude Code
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = H levels (degenerate). CRIMSON = Na levels (split). INK for all Text.

beats.narration_text: Medhavy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude — draw an energy level diagram comparing hydrogen and sodium for the n-equals-three shell. Hydrogen: all three subshells at minus thirteen-point-six over nine electron-volts — degenerate. Sodium: three-s at minus five-point-one, three-p at minus three-point-zero electron-volts — split by screening. Label the gap. Specify: both atoms, the degeneracy and the splitting.

props.command:
claude "draw n=3 energy level diagram:
  H:  E(3s)=E(3p)=E(3d) = -13.6/9 = -1.51 eV  [degenerate]
  Na: E(3s) = -5.1 eV  E(3p) = -3.0 eV  [split]
  label gap Δ = 2.1 eV"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes it. Read before running. Two checks. First: the hydrogen column. All three subshells must point to the same line — one energy, minus one-point-five-one electron-volts. If three-s, three-p, and three-d land at different heights, the code forgot that hydrogen has no l-dependence. Second: the gap for sodium. It should read two-point-one electron-volts. If it reads zero, the screening was not applied. Thirty seconds. Catch it before the render.

props.code:
# orbitals.py  —  Claude Code output
# Hydrogen n=3 (Coulomb: no ℓ-dependence)
E_H = -13.6 / 9    # = -1.511 eV  (all 3s,3p,3d)

# Sodium n=3 (experimental orbital energies)
E_Na_3s = -5.1     # eV  (3s sees more Z_eff)
E_Na_3p = -3.0     # eV  (3p shielded more)
gap = E_Na_3s - E_Na_3p  # = -2.1 eV  ✓

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two checks. First: the hydrogen column. All three teal lines — three-s, three-p, three-d — land at exactly the same height: minus one-point-five-one electron-volts. One energy for all three. Coulomb degeneracy. Second: the sodium column. The crimson three-s line is lower than three-p. The gap reads two-point-one electron-volts. Screening breaks the degeneracy — and the periodic table is built on that break.

Iterate: claude — update orbitals dot py — add a double-headed arrow between three-s and three-p in the sodium column, label it gap equals two-point-one electron-volts. One diff, one re-run. Prompt, read, run, check, change. The orbital diagram was the vehicle. The loop is the takeaway.

props.command:
claude "update orbitals.py:
  → add arrow between Na 3s and 3p
  → label Δ = 2.1 eV"

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational A-I. On YouTube, at Nik Bear Brown. Nik Bear Brown dot com.
